evals.prompt: Here are my lecture notes on K-Nearest Neighbors (plain text). Make practice questions for my students.
evals.expected_output: Generates all four question types in order: True/False (one per learning objective or 3-5), Explanatory (3-5 questions), Coding (1 KNN implementation question with function signature and steps), Use Case (1 realistic scenario). No answer key included. Output in plain text.

evals.prompt: Can you turn this lecture PDF on regularization (L1/L2) into a LaTeX practice exam I can hand out?
evals.expected_output: Reads assets/questions_template.tex and uses it as skeleton. Extracts learning objectives and topics from regularization lecture. Generates all four question types. Output is valid LaTeX using the template structure. No answer key.

evals.prompt: I have a lecture on photosynthesis (Markdown file). I need some study questions for my biology students.
evals.expected_output: Generates all four question types appropriate to biology content. Does NOT read examples_by_topic.md (ML-only). If user requests Markdown output, reads markdown_template.md. No answer key. Examples and scenarios are biology-specific, not ML.

evals.prompt: Make practice questions from this Decision Trees lecture. The lecture has explicit learning objectives listed at the top.
evals.expected_output: Detects learning objectives at top of lecture. Generates one True/False per learning objective (not a fixed 3-5). Reads examples_by_topic.md for ML-calibrated examples since content is ML. Generates all four question types. No answer key.
